metrics.narrative: The buyer articulated clear operational pain but never quantified it. On 29 Oct 2025 Jonas Elber said the majority of purchase orders are submitted as free text, 'so it's difficult to me, as responsible for the spend, to see what are we spending our money on,' and on 20 Nov 2025 framed P2P as a process problem because 'procurement owns this process but we have owners in finance who owns the PTP process and we don't talk to each other.' The CLM driver is 10,000 contracts stored in SharePoint that are 'difficult to manage' (20 Nov 2025). Business_Objectives lists compliance, risk, auditability and IFRS support but attaches no cost, headcount, cycle-time or savings figure. No ROI or savings model has been built. Quantifying the value case (spend leakage from free-text orders, contract-cycle time, legal review hours) is the open move so the board decides on value, not price — especially important against two unnamed S2P finalists who may undercut.

sources: Avoma P2P demo part 2 2025-11-27 (Nickolaj Almskou Holler)
champion.narrative: Jonas Elber is a strong, active champion and his position has strengthened. He owns the CLM recommendation, declined a CLM RFP because he is already driving it, drove the demo agenda and stakeholder list, publicly rated Zycus ahead on AI, and was promoted to Head of Technology on 29 May 2026 while 'preparing the business case.' The gap is a second senior advocate at the board/CFO level: Nickolaj Almskou Holler (Director, Global Business Services & Transformation) requested references, pricing and sandbox on 27 Nov 2025 but has not re-surfaced on a captured call, so the bridge to the board is not yet locked.

competition.narrative: This is a formal ITTStc 3-vendor S2P evaluation (17 Mar 2026) plus incumbent displacement (SAP ECC/Bear Shop, SharePoint, Adobe Sign). The two peer S2P finalists are unnamed — Competitors__c is blank and Jonas disclosed no names ('we are meeting vendors to see what they can offer'). The future-state ERP path is undecided (Dynamics-leaning), making dual-ERP support a live lever. Our wedge is AI maturity (Merlin agents, NegoGenie, contract risk monitor), which Jonas himself rated ahead of competition. The risk is a Dynamics-native or lower-priced peer reaching the board with a stronger business case. Naming the other two finalists directly with Jonas is an open move.

sources: Avoma CLM demo part 2 2025-11-25 (Kristine Green Møller, Jonas Elber)
identify_pain.narrative: Pain is clearly articulated and owned. On P2P, the custom 'Bear Shop' app on aging SAP ECC is 'error-prone, has a lot of limitations, and cannot support dual ERP solutions' and is slated for decommissioning in 2026, creating a replacement need; free-text ordering means Jonas cannot see or control spend. On CLM, 10,000 contracts sit in SharePoint with inconsistent, manual, person-dependent processes (Kristine: 'we don't have a common process, we each do it differently'). Pain owners are Jonas Elber and the finance system owner. Change management within the legal team is a named adoption risk Jonas raised himself.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are partially visible. An NDA was signed earlier. Next_Step_History 23 Jan 2026 notes 'Legal & Infosec planned in parallel subject to P2P decision' — legal and security review sequenced after vendor selection. Partner E&Y is named (19 Nov 2025) and may shape contracting/implementation, though Jonas has since asked for Zycus-delivered implementation pricing with no partner (17 Mar 2026). No MSA/SOW redlines are in progress and no contract-expiration forcing function exists. Visibility would improve by agreeing a written mutual close plan covering pricing sign-off, legal, InfoSec and signature steps once the board recommendation lands.

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is named by role but never engaged. On 25 Nov 2025 Jonas Elber stated 'the recommendation that the team does need to go to an IT and Digital Board where our CFO is involved... making the final decision of each of these projects,' and the 17 Mar 2026 ITTStc cadence confirms the same board owns the approval. Neither the CFO nor any board member has appeared on a call, and Executive_Sponsor_Identified is false. Jonas owns the CLM recommendation and his 29 May promotion to Head of Technology raises his weight, but he does not have unilateral authority. Because this is a non-forecasted deal in band terms, the route to power is THROUGH the champion: ask Jonas to broker a short CFO/board pre-brief (pain, ROI, AI wedge, timeline) before the recommendation deck is finalized — do not cold-approach the CFO.

decision_process.narrative: The process is mapped. Jonas confirmed no formal RFP on the CLM ('I'm already involved'), but the P2P side became a formal ITTStc 3-vendor S2P evaluation (17 Mar 2026: 'ITTStc approved we continue the P2P evaluation based on 3 vendors with a S2P focus. Vendor selection end of April and recommendation/approval... end of May/beg June'). Approval routes through the IT and Digital Board where the CFO is involved, decided per project. Next_Step_History shows real progression: Steering Committee (Jan 2026), 'CLM vendor selected, P2P undecided' (23 Jan), 'P2P approved - meeting with Head of Finance' (12 Mar), commercial/business-case work into May, then a year-end pause. The process is live but has slipped past its original May/June gate.

sources: Avoma CLM demo 2025-11-21 (Kristine Green Møller, Jonas Elber)
decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are well evidenced. AI maturity is the explicit differentiator: on 25 Nov 2025 Jonas said 'this is where it becomes interesting... you already have this incorporated and in my view have come quite far on it compared to competition, so you are in a good position.' Legal (Kristine Green Møller) pressed on Word-based redlining, audit trail, executive-summary visibility to external signees, and AI hallucination control via multi-LLM orchestration. Technical criteria include dual-ERP support (SAP ECC plus likely Microsoft Dynamics), migration of 10,000 SharePoint contracts with AI metadata extraction, catalog adoption to reduce free-text spend, and data privacy. The unresolved criterion is Outlook contract-email capture without scanning every inbox, raised by legal as an error/compliance risk.

The buyer is AI-hungry (stated enthusiasm for Merlin, Negogenie, AP Smartdesk, contract risk monitor) and Zycus is positioned ahead of the unnamed peer vendors in the evaluation. The commercial proposal (due overdue as of 2026-06-26) must lead with the relevant stakeholder: quantify time saved via invoice AI, contract discovery, and redlining automation; include AI maturity benchmarking vs competitors; and anchor the Merlin agent story to the buyer's May 2026 implementation urgency. The reference videos (also overdue) should feature customers who adopted Merlin and saw measurable procurement efficiency gains within 6 months. If a named peer competitor (Coupa, GEP, Ivalua, Jaggaer, SAP Ariba) surfaces in the next meeting, tailor the AI narrative to kill their specific gaps (e.g., Coupa's invoice AI is weaker; SAP Ariba has no Negogenie equivalent).

Lock the IT and Digital Board presentation date for July-Aug and arm Jonas with a decision-grade board deck. Jonas committed 25 Nov to January 2026 contract signature—7 months past. The board presentation is the final gate and it is not yet scheduled. To close by 30 Nov 2026 requires board decision in July-Aug, contract signed by end Aug, and implementation 3 months. Action: on the commercial review call, ask Jonas to lock the board presentation date (target: mid-July to mid-Aug) and offer to co-author the board deck with him (one-pager: pain, ROI, AI wedge, timeline, risk mitigation). Goal: give Jonas a decision-grade artifact he can present with confidence, and force the timeline by anchoring the board date.

Competitive blind spot: this is a named 3-vendor ITTStc bake-off but the two peers are unknown — ask Jonas directly who else is in and their ERP-native vs AI-first positioning to defend the Merlin wedge.
Champion just gained power (promoted to Head of Technology, building the business case) — co-author a quantified ROI model now so his board deck wins on value, not just price or Dynamics-fit.
best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy priorities: pre-sell the CFO/board, quantify the value case, and unblock the two legal/commercial threads holding up the proposal.

competitive_position.summary: A formal ITTStc 3-vendor S2P evaluation with two unnamed peer finalists, layered on incumbent displacement (SAP ECC/Bear Shop, SharePoint, Adobe Sign). The strongest live dynamic is the undecided ERP path (Dynamics-leaning), which makes dual-ERP support an evaluation lever; Zycus's defensible edge is AI maturity, which Jonas rated ahead of competition on 25 Nov 2025. The unresolved threat is a Dynamics-native or cheaper peer reaching the board with a stronger business case while our pricing is still outstanding.

deltas.subject: Unnamed peer Source-to-Pay vendors are in the evaluation. Jonas confirmed on 2025-11-20 'we really need to see what is out on the market' and on 2025-11-27 stated he is 'meeting vendors to see what they can offer.' No competitor names (Coupa, GEP, Ivalua, Jaggaer, SAP Ariba) were disclosed on any call. Jonas positioned Zycus ahead on AI maturity (2025-11-25), but the final competitive field is unknown. If a peer competitor with strong ERP integration (e.g., SAP Ariba if they stay SAP, or a Dynamics-native P2P player) emerges as the other finalist, Zycus's AI wedge may not be enough to win if the competitor matches on ERP-native positioning and undercuts on price.

deltas.to: Push the AI differentiation wedge in the commercial proposal and reference calls. Jonas Elber confirmed on 2025-11-25 'you are in a good position' because 'you
deltas.subject: Push the AI differentiation wedge in the commercial proposal and reference calls. Jonas Elber confirmed on 2025-11-25 'you are in a good position' because 'you already have this incorporated and in my view, have come quite far on it compared to competition.' The buyer is AI-hungry (stated enthusiasm for Merlin, Negogenie, AP Smartdesk, contract risk monitor) and Zycus is positioned ahead of the unnamed peer vendors in the evaluation. The commercial proposal (due overdue as of 2026-06-26) must lead with the relevant stakeholder: quantify time saved via invoice AI, contract discovery, and redlining automation; include AI maturity benchmarking vs competitors; and anchor the Merlin agent story to the buyer's May 2026 implementation urgency. The reference videos (also overdue) should feature customers who adopted Merlin and saw measurable procurement efficiency gains within 6 months. If a named peer competitor (Coupa, GEP, Ivalua, Jaggaer, SAP Ariba) surfaces in the next meeting, tailor the AI narrative to kill their specific gaps (e.g., Coupa's invoice AI is weaker; SAP Ariba has no Negogenie equivalent).

deltas.to: Secure the CFO-led IT and Digital Board presentation slot in December 2025 to meet the January 2026 contract signature deadline. Jonas Elber stated on 2025-11-2
deltas.subject: Secure the CFO-led IT and Digital Board presentation slot in December 2025 to meet the January 2026 contract signature deadline. Jonas Elber stated on 2025-11-25 'the recommendation that the team does need to go to an IT and digital board where our CFO is involved' and 'we would need a decision in January, a contract signed in January, for us to meet those deadlines' (May 2026 go-live). Anthony Gray committed to a December 9-10 in-person meeting in Denmark to 'ratify any of the final bits and pieces, on the state of works, understanding timelines.' The next 30 days (July 2026) are critical: (1) deliver the overdue commercials, sandbox, reference videos, and Outlook integration email by early July, (2) schedule the December Denmark meeting (Copenhagen or Jutland, Jonas will confirm location) by mid-July, (3) co-author the board presentation deck with Jonas in that December meeting (CFO-facing: AI ROI, dual-ERP risk mitigation, May 2026 timeline feasibility, peer customer proof), and (4) secure the board slot for late December or early January. If the board presentation slips past January, the May 2026 implementation is at risk and Jonas's internal credibility erodes. The deal owner's manager (Anthony Gray, already committed) should co-present with Jonas to the CFO, positioning Zycus as a strategic procurement transformation partner, not a software vendor.

deltas.to: Convert Nickolaj Almskou Holler from evaluator to co-champion via sandbox access and peer validation. Nickolaj (Director, Global Business Services & Transformat
deltas.subject: Convert Nickolaj Almskou Holler from evaluator to co-champion via sandbox access and peer validation. Nickolaj (Director, Global Business Services & Transformation, Contact Role) emerged on 2025-11-27 as an active validator: he requested sandbox/POC access, reference customer videos, and ballpark pricing, signaling he will influence the IT and Digital Board recommendation alongside Jonas. He is transformation-focused (likely owns the Dynamics migration and dual-ERP orchestration) and needs hands-on proof that Zycus can handle the SAP ECC + Dynamics parallel run without implementation risk. Action: (1) deliver the sandbox within 14 days (by mid-July 2026) with a pre-configured dual-ERP scenario (SAP ECC + Dynamics mock data, auto-routed line items, separate PO numbers per supplier as demoed 2025-11-21); (2) schedule a 90-minute sandbox walkthrough with Nickolaj and the finance team (Mikkel, Mark) to let them test invoice capture, budget controls, and three-way matching hands-on; (3) provide three reference videos featuring customers who completed a dual-ERP P2P implementation in under 6 months (address Nickolaj's timeline anxiety). If Nickolaj validates the platform and co-sponsors the board presentation with Jonas, the deal accelerates to Vendor Selected. If he stays neutral or raises implementation risk concerns, the decision stalls or a competitor wins on 'safer' ERP-native positioning.
